SEARCH
Package neqsim.process.equipment.compressor

Class CompressorChartCalibrator

java.lang.Object
neqsim.process.equipment.compressor.CompressorChartCalibrator
public class CompressorChartCalibrator extends Object
Utility to calibrate a compressor performance chart from field data.

This helper supports the anti-surge / surge-control-line workflow where a screening compressor chart (for example one generated by CompressorChartGenerator) must be brought closer to reality using two field measurements:

  • a surge test that yields measured surge points (flow, head) — used by fitSurgeCurve(double[], double[]) to replace the theoretical surge curve; and
  • a gas sample that yields the actual molecular weight — used by molarMassHeadCorrectionFactor(double, double) to correct the polytropic head for the change in gas properties by similarity (H_p is proportional to 1 / MW at fixed volumetric flow and speed).

The class also provides a simple recommendControlMargin(double, double[]) helper that widens a base control-line margin by the relative scatter of the measured surge points, so a noisier surge test produces a more conservative control line.

Results are screening-level and must be reviewed by a qualified rotating-equipment engineer (API 617 / API 692) and confirmed against the vendor performance test (ASME PTC 10 / ISO 5389) before use.

Version:
1.0
Author:
NeqSim

  • Constructor Details

    • CompressorChartCalibrator

      public CompressorChartCalibrator(Compressor compressor)
      Create a calibrator bound to a compressor.
      Parameters:
      compressor - the compressor whose chart is to be calibrated (must not be null)

  • Method Details

    • fitSurgeCurve

      public void fitSurgeCurve(double[] flow, double[] head)
      Replace the surge curve of the compressor chart with one fitted to measured surge points.

      The flow and head arrays are the coordinates of the surge points recorded during a field surge test (inlet actual volumetric flow in m3/hr and polytropic head in the chart head unit). A spline-based surge curve with safe extrapolation (SafeSplineSurgeCurve) is built and installed on the compressor chart.

      Parameters:
      flow - measured surge flow points (m3/hr); must have the same length as head and at least one point
      head - measured surge head points (same head unit as the chart)
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the arrays are null, of different length, or empty

    • molarMassHeadCorrectionFactor

      public static double molarMassHeadCorrectionFactor(double mwReference, double mwActual)
      Head correction factor for a change in gas molecular weight.

      At a fixed inlet volumetric flow and speed the polytropic head of a centrifugal compressor is inversely proportional to the gas molecular weight (H_p proportional to Z R T1 / MW). Multiplying a reference-MW head by this factor gives the head at the actual MW.

      Parameters:
      mwReference - the reference molecular weight the map was built for (g/mol or kg/mol; units cancel)
      mwActual - the actual molecular weight from the gas sample (same unit as mwReference)
      Returns:
      the head correction factor mwReference / mwActual
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if either molecular weight is not strictly positive

    • recommendControlMargin

      public double recommendControlMargin(double baseMargin, double[] measuredSurgeFlow)
      Recommend an anti-surge control-line margin from a base margin and the scatter of the measured surge points.

      The recommended margin is baseMargin + coefficientOfVariation(flow), where the coefficient of variation is the standard deviation of the measured surge flows divided by their mean. A noisier surge test therefore yields a wider (more conservative) control-line margin. A single measured point contributes zero scatter.

      Parameters:
      baseMargin - the minimum control-line margin (fraction of surge flow, e.g. 0.10)
      measuredSurgeFlow - the measured surge flow points (m3/hr)
      Returns:
      the recommended control-line margin as a fraction of surge flow
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if baseMargin is negative or the array is null/empty
